'''Ethan O'Neill''' (born 1 June 1999) is an [[Australia]]n professional [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|sr}} forward for [[Hull FC]], on loan from the [[Leeds Rhinos]] in the [[Betfred Super League]].

He has previously played for the [[Leigh Leopards]] in the [[Super League]]. O'Neill was contracted to the [[South Sydney Rabbitohs]] and the [[Brisbane Broncos]] in the [[NRL]]. He has also played for the [[Tweed Heads Seagulls]], [[Central Queensland Capras]] and the [[Burleigh Bears]] in the [[Queensland Cup]], as well as [[South Sydney Rabbitohs|South Sydney]] in the [[New South Wales Cup]].<ref>[https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/cde7grpgk9no Leigh sign Australian forward O'Neill]</ref><ref>[https://www.itsrugby.co.uk/players/ethan-oneill-63618.html itsrugby profile]</ref> He played as a {{rlp|ce}} earlier in his career.

==Background==
O'Neill was born in [[Randwick]], in the Eastern Suburbs of Sydney, New South Wales, Australia. He grew up in [[Maroubra, New South Wales]] and in [[Widnes, Cheshire]], England.<ref>[https://www.patreon.com/posts/i-do-all-this-my-128540241 'I do all this for my family': O'Neill motivated by tragedy and history]</ref> He is the son of former professional rugby league footballer [[Julian O'Neill]].<ref>[https://www.leighjournal.co.uk/sport/24716902.leigh-leopards-sign-julian-oneills-son-ethan-oneill/ Leigh Leopards sign Julian O'Neill's son Ethan O'Neill]</ref>

He played for the [[Clovelly Crocodiles]] as a junior where he was coached by his future [[Leigh Leopards|Leigh]] coach [[Adrian Lam]].<ref>[https://www.leighjournal.co.uk/sport/24774109.ethan-oneill-looking-add-bite-leigh-leopards-pack/ Ethan O'Neill looking to add bite to Leigh Leopards pack]</ref> He progressed through the [[Sydney Roosters]] junior system, playing in their [[Harold Matthews Cup|Harold Matthews]], [[S. G. Ball Cup|SG Ball]] and [[Jersey Flegg Cup|Jersey Flegg]] sides between 2015 and 2019.<ref>[https://18thman.com/players/ethan-oneill/ 18TH MAN - Ethan O’Neill]</ref>

O'Neill played for the Queensland under-16 team.<ref>[https://www.qrl.com.au/news/2015/05/05/queensland-u16-team/ Queensland U16 team]</ref><ref>{{Cite web |last=Weidler |first=Danny |date=2015-05-09 |title=Julian O'Neill in Facebook spat with Jack Elsegood over son Ethan |url=https://www.smh.com.au/sport/nrl/julian-oneill-in-facebook-spat-with-jack-elsegood-over-son-ethan-20150509-ggy0d8.html |access-date=2024-06-07 |website=The Sydney Morning Herald |language=en}}</ref>

==Career==
O'Neill played in the [[Sydney Roosters|Roosters]] under 20s side in 2018 and 2019 before moving to the [[Tweed Heads Seagulls]] ahead of the [[2021 Queensland Cup]] season.<ref>[https://www.qrl.com.au/news/2020/03/09/tweed-seagulls-look-to-their-youth-in-season-2020/ Tweed Seagulls look to their youth in season 2020]</ref>

He made his debut in March 2021 for the [[Tweed Heads Seagulls|Tweed Seagulls]] against the [[Wynnum Manly Seagulls]] in the [[Queensland Cup]]. He played 4 games for the [[Tweed Heads Seagulls|Tweed]] before leaving for the [[Central Queensland Capras]].<ref>[https://www.therhinos.co.uk/article/22594/get-to-know-ethan-oneill Get to know... Ethan O'Neill]</ref>

O'Neill made his [[Central Queensland Capras|Capras]] debut in July 2021.<ref>[https://www.couriermail.com.au/news/queensland/rockhampton/sport/capras-edged-by-cutters-in-xxxx-rivalry-round/news-story/99338a794f9e89c31a0b449e5bdb5ce8 Capras edged by Cutters in XXXX Rivalry Round]</ref> He played 25 games over the course of a one and a half year stay at [[Browne Park]], before returning to [[New South Wales]] to join the [[South Sydney Rabbitohs|Rabbitohs]] ahead of the [[2023 NSW Cup season]].<ref>[https://www.leighjournal.co.uk/sport/leighcenturions/centurions_match_report/25618233.leigh-leopards-forward-ethan-oneill-joins-leeds-rhinos/ Leigh Leopards forward Ethan O'Neill joins Leeds Rhinos]</ref>

He played in a [[NRL]] trial match for the South Sydney Rabbitohs in February 2023. O'Neill played 25 games over the course of the season and in September 2023 won the [[2023 NSW Cup season#Grand Final|2023 NSW Cup Grand Final]] with victory over the [[North Sydney Bears]].<ref>[https://www.nswrl.com.au/draw/nsw-cup/2023/grand-final/rabbitohs-v-bears/ Rabbitohs 22-18 Bears]</ref> In October 2023 he scored a try in winning the [[2023 NRL State Championship]], with victory over the [[Brisbane Tigers]].<ref>[https://www.nrl.com/draw/nrl-state-championship/2023/nrl-state-championship/rabbitohs-v-tigers/ Rabbitohs 42-22 Tigers]</ref>

===Brisbane Broncos===
After a highly successful 2023 O'Neill moved to the [[Brisbane Broncos]] in the [[National Rugby League]]. He featured in one NRL trial match in 2024, but did not play in first-grade.<ref>[https://www.alloutrugbyleague.co.uk/news/leigh-leopards-super-league-hunt-1384921 Leigh Leopards star eyes NRL and Origin dream after Super League hunt]</ref>

He spent all of the 2025 season playing in the [[Queensland Cup]] for the [[Burleigh Bears]], as a contracted player with the [[Brisbane Broncos]].<ref>[https://www.yorkshireeveningpost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/leeds-rhinos-news-ethan-oneill-makes-something-special-vow-explains-move-from-leigh-leopards-5431221 Leeds Rhinos news: Ethan O'Neill makes 'something special' vow, explains move from Leigh Leopards]</ref>

===Leigh Leopards===
O'Neill joined the [[Leigh Leopards]] in the [[Super League]] on a two-year deal ahead of the [[2025 Super League season]].<ref>[https://www.totalrl.com/ex-south-sydney-rabbitohs-second-rower-ethan-oneill-makes-super-league-move/ Ex-South Sydney Rabbitohs second-rower Ethan O’Neill makes Super League move]</ref>

===Leeds Rhinos===
He left the [[Leigh Leopards|Leopards]] to join the [[Leeds Rhinos]] ahead of the [[2026 Super League season]].<ref>[https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/ethan-oneill-joins-leeds-ahead-of-2026 Ethan O’Neill’s 172-word message as Leeds move and Leigh exit announced]</ref>

===Hull FC (loan)===
On 10 May 2026 it was reported that he had signed for [[Hull FC]] in the [[Super League]] on loan<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=10 May 2026|author=Matthew Shaw|title=Hull FC secure Leeds Rhinos duo on loan with Harry Newman recalled|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/hull-fc-secure-leeds-rhinos-duo-on-loan-with-harry-newman-recalled}}</ref>
